rule_content:
RULE "SEMANTIC-DUPLICATE-HERMESPARSEROPTIONS" : IArchitecture
  ID: "AR-SEMANTIC-DUPLICATE-001"
  DESCRIPTION: "Semantic duplicate detected: HermesParserOptions in different scopes"
  SEVERITY: Warning
  CATEGORY: "Code_Quality"
  APPLIES_TO: ["csharp"]
  PLUGIN:
    ID: "custom"
    SCOPE: "codebase"
    CONFIG:
      similarity_threshold: "0.95"
      types_to_check: "[\"eslint-plugin-react-compiler.HermesParserOptions\", \"eslint-plugin-react-hooks.HermesParserOptions\"]"
      message: "Semantic duplication detected in HermesParserOptions across different scopes"

  WHY_IT_MATTERS: "Identical or nearly identical code structures across different scopes can lead to maintenance challenges, increased code size, and potential bugs. This rule helps enforce DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principles by identifying semantic duplicates."
  BUSINESS_IMPACT: "Violating this rule can result in increased development time, higher maintenance costs, and potential security risks due to inconsistencies."
  QUICK_FIX: "Refactor the duplicate code into a shared abstraction or utility function. For example, create a common library that both scopes can use."

  ARCHITECTURAL_CONTEXT:
    PROJECT_TYPE: "REACT_LIBRARY"
    DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: ["DRY", "Code_Simplicity"]

  NOTE: "Proposed by IArchitecture Governance Intelligence (CA-DRY-001, 2026-04-20). Cluster 40493f64 — 2 types, purity 0.50, cohesion 0.96, confidence 98%."
anomaly_description:
Anomaly type: SemanticDuplicate
Dominant scope: eslint-plugin-react-compiler / layer: Base
Cluster size: 2 types
Purity: 0.50  Cohesion: 0.96
Two or more types in different scopes have nearly identical embedding vectors — possible DRY violation or shared concept without shared abstraction.
Sample members: eslint-plugin-react-compiler.HermesParserOptions, eslint-plugin-react-hooks.HermesParserOptions

Confidence signals: 2 signal(s) agree (geometric + name similarity) -- confidence 98%

rule_content:
RULE "UNEXPECTED-COUPLING-DETECTION" : IArchitecture  
ID: "ARCH-UNEXPECTED-COUPLING-001"  
DESCRIPTION: "Unexpected coupling detected between React modules - creates hidden dependencies and misplaced responsibilities"  
SEVERITY: Warning  
CATEGORY: "Architecture_Quality"  
APPLIES_TO: ["javascript", "typescript"]  

PLUGIN:  
  ID: "coupling-metrics-validator"  
  SCOPE: "codebase"  
  CONFIG:  
    purity_threshold: "0.5"  
    cohesion_threshold: "0.6"  

WHY_IT_MATTERS: "Unexpected coupling between modules can lead to hidden dependencies, making the code harder to understand, maintain, and refactor. It can also result in misplaced responsibilities that violate architectural boundaries."  
BUSINESS_IMPACT: "Hidden coupling increases technical debt and can slow down development velocity. Refactoring becomes more complex as the codebase grows, potentially leading to unintended side effects or breaking changes."  
QUICK_FIX: "Refactor the coupled modules into separate components or layers. Ensure clear separation of responsibilities and document any inter-module dependencies clearly."  

ARCHITECTURAL_CONTEXT:  
PROJECT_TYPE: "UI_Development_Tools"  
DESIGN_PRINCIPLES: ["Low_Coupling", "High_Cohesion", "Modular_Architecture"]  
NOTE: "Proposed by IArchitecture Governance Intelligence (CA-COUPLING-001, 2026-04-20). Cluster 58cfcb05 — 94 types, purity 0.26, cohesion 0.80, confidence 31%."
anomaly_description:
Anomaly type: UnexpectedCoupling
Dominant scope: react-devtools-timeline / layer: DevTools
Cluster size: 94 types
Purity: 0.26  Cohesion: 0.80
Mixed scopes: react, react-art, react-client, react-devtools-extensions, react-devtools-shared, react-devtools-shell, react-devtools-timeline, react-dom-bindings
Types from these scopes cluster together semantically — hidden coupling or misplaced responsibility.
Sample members: react.helpers, react.pattern, react.Postpone, react.property, react-art.LinearGradient, react-art.Pattern

Confidence signals: 3 signal(s) agree (geometric + layer violations + circular dependency) -- confidence 31%
